signal_title: Consultation on Protecting Media Service Providers on Online Platforms
risk_rationale: The draft bill introduces a requirement for platforms to notify media providers before content removal and allows a 24-hour response window. This is a moderate risk as it requires procedural changes but does not pose an existential threat or immediate high-impact compliance gap.

compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Platforms must notify media providers before content removal with a 24-hour response window.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The baseline does not currently require notification to media providers before content removal, nor does it allow a 24-hour response window.

remediations.title: Implement Media Provider Notification System
remediations.description: Develop a notification system to inform media providers of potential content removal, allowing them a 24-hour window to respond before action is taken.
acceptance_criteria: Media providers receive notifications before content removal.
Media providers have a 24-hour window to respond to notifications.

business_impact: The requirement to notify media providers and allow a response window could delay content moderation processes, potentially impacting the platform's ability to quickly address harmful content.
recommended_actions: Assess the technical feasibility of implementing a notification system for media providers.
Engage with EU regulators to clarify the scope and applicability of the new requirements.
Prepare a communication plan for media providers to explain the new notification process.
